---
title: "Week 11"
description: "Final Exam Review and Final Exam"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

```{=html}
<!--
Think of the weekly page as answering just five student questions:
    1.  What are we doing this week?
    2.  What do I need to read/watch before class?
    3.  What materials should I have open during class?
    4.  What is due (and when)?
    5.  Where do I go if I want to review later?
    -->
```

## Announcements

-   **The final exam opens Monday, March 16 at 3:00 PM and closes Friday, March 20 at 5:00 PM**
-   No new homework this week

## Overview

This is the final week of the course. Monday is dedicated to exam review — we will go through key concepts and R workflows from across the semester, with emphasis on Lessons 10–19. Bring your questions.

Wednesday's session is optional. There is no new material; it is an open Q&A for anyone who wants to talk through the exam or revisit anything before the deadline. Attendance is not required and will not affect your grade.

By the end of the week, you should feel confident in your ability to:

-   select the appropriate statistical test for a given research question and data structure,
-   apply the six-step hypothesis testing framework across all test types covered this semester,
-   check and interpret assumptions for parametric and nonparametric tests,
-   fit and interpret a simple linear regression model in R,
-   distinguish between confidence intervals for mean response and prediction intervals, and
-   communicate statistical results clearly in context.

## Readings (before class)

-   No required reading for Monday

## Materials

### Monday — Final Exam Review

-   **Topics**
    -   Exam logistics and structure
    -   First-half foundations: study design, Bayes' theorem, normal distributions and CLT
    -   Paired t-test
    -   One-way ANOVA and post-hoc testing
    -   Categorical data analysis: chi-squared and two-proportion tests
    -   Nonparametric tests: Wilcoxon and Kruskal-Wallis
    -   Correlation and simple linear regression
    -   Power, sample size, and choosing the right test
-   **Slides**
    -   Final Exam Review <!-- ([HTML](/lessons/22_final_review/22_final_review.qmd) \| [PDF]() \| [OneDrive]()) -->
    
-   **Recording**
    -   Coming soon.
-   **Muddy points**
    -   Coming soon.



------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Wednesday — Optional Q&A Session

-   Attendance is **optional** — no new material will be covered
-   Come with questions about the exam, specific concepts, or R code
-   **Recording**
    -   Recording will be posted if the session is held

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Final Exam

**Available:** Wednesday, March 16 at 3:00 PM through Friday, March 20 at 5:00 PM

**Access:** [Sakai](https://sakai.ohsu.edu/)

**Time to budget:** 4–6 hours (you do not need to complete it in one sitting)

### Submission Requirements

Upload **both** files to Sakai:

1.  Your `.qmd` source file
2.  The rendered `.html` file

**File naming:** `Lastname_FirstInitial_Final` (e.g., `Smith_J_Final.qmd` and `Smith_J_Final.html`)

### Important Reminders

-   **Open book/notes:** You may use course materials, R documentation, and your notes
-   **No collaboration:** Work independently — no help from other students, AI assistants, or online forums
-   **Check before submitting:** Make sure your document renders to HTML without errors

### Resources

-   Final Exam Review <!-- ([HTML]() \| [PDF]() \| [OneDrive]()) -->
-   See [Schedule](schedule.qmd) for all lecture materials and homework

## Post-class check-in (attendance)

-   Please complete the post-class check-in after Monday's class meeting.
-   Wednesday attendance is optional and no check-in is required.
-   Link: [Post-class check-in](https://forms.gle/AMX1S1vCGEEfmwLY6)
